title: bupleurum falcatum subsp. alpigenum
intro: Bupleurum falcatum subsp. alpigenum is a subspecies of the Bupleurum falcatum plant, also known as the Chinese thorowax or hare's ear, a perennial herb native to East Asia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, and is traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Bupleurum falcatum subsp. alpigenum include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of menopausal disorders, as well as its potential to lower cholesterol levels and protect against cardiovascular disease. In herbal medicine, Bupleurum falcatum subsp. alpigenum is often prepared as a decoction, tincture, or tea, and is used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
